dustbin.en: a container for holding waste until it is thrown away (British English)

ex.en: She threw the wrapper into the dustbin.

dusty.en: covered with a layer of dust

ex.en: The old books were dusty and forgotten.
ex.ko: 그 오래된 책들은 먼지가 쌓이고 잊혀져 있었다.

duty.en: something you must do because it is morally or legally right

ex.en: It's every citizen's duty to vote.

duty.en: a tax you must pay on goods brought into a country

ex.en: You have to pay duty on items over a certain value.
ex.ko: 일정 금액이 넘는 물건에는 관세를 내야 한다.

duty-free.en: sold without the usual government tax

ex.en: She bought duty-free perfume at the airport.

duvet.en: a thick soft quilt used as a bed cover

ex.en: He pulled the duvet over his head to keep warm.
ex.ko: 그는 따뜻하게 있으려고 이불을 머리 위까지 끌어올렸다.

dynasty.en: a series of rulers from the same family

ex.en: The Joseon Dynasty ruled Korea for over five hundred years.
ex.ko: 조선 왕조는 오백 년 넘게 한국을 다스렸다.

eager.en: wanting very much to do or have something

ex.en: She was eager to start her new job.
ex.ko: 그녀는 새 직장을 몹시 시작하고 싶어 했다.

eagerness.en: a strong feeling of wanting to do something very much

ex.en: He answered the question with great eagerness.
ex.ko: 그는 큰 열의를 가지고 그 질문에 답했다.

ex.en: My son has had an earache since yesterday.
ex.ko: 내 아들은 어제부터 귀앓이를 하고 있다.

earnest.en: serious and sincere about what you say or do

ex.en: He made an earnest attempt to apologize.

eastern.en: in or from the east of an area

ex.en: They live in eastern Europe.

easygoing.en: relaxed and not easily worried or annoyed

ex.en: My grandfather has an easygoing personality.
ex.ko: 우리 할아버지는 느긋한 성격을 가지고 있다.

eco.en: relating to being kind to the environment (informal, short for ecological/eco-friendly)

ex.en: The shop only sells eco products.

ecological.en: relating to the relationship between living things and their environment

ex.en: The oil spill caused an ecological disaster.
ex.ko: 그 기름 유출은 생태학적 재앙을 일으켰다.

ecology.en: the scientific study of how living things relate to their environment

ex.en: She is studying ecology at university.

economic.en: relating to trade, industry, and money

ex.en: The country is facing an economic crisis.
ex.ko: 그 나라는 경제 위기를 겪고 있다.

economics.en: the study of how money, goods, and services are produced and used

ex.en: He majored in economics in college.

economy.en: the system of trade and industry in a country

ex.en: The national economy is growing steadily.

economy.en: careful use of money or resources to avoid waste

ex.en: We need to practice economy while prices are high.

ecosystem.en: all the living things in an area and how they relate to their environment

ex.en: Coral reefs support a rich ecosystem.

ecstasy.en: a feeling of extreme happiness or excitement

ex.en: She screamed in ecstasy when she won the prize.
ex.ko: 그녀는 상을 받고 황홀감에 소리를 질렀다.

edge.en: the outer or furthest part of something

ex.en: She stood at the edge of the cliff.

edge.en: a slight advantage over someone else

ex.en: Their experience gives them the edge in the competition.
ex.ko: 그들의 경험이 그 경쟁에서 우위를 준다.

edition.en: one of the printed forms of a book, magazine, or newspaper

ex.en: This is the third edition of the textbook.
ex.ko: 이것은 그 교과서의 세 번째 판이다.

educate.en: teach someone over a period of time so they gain knowledge

ex.en: Parents want to educate their children well.

effective.en: producing the result that you want

ex.en: This medicine is very effective against headaches.

effective.en: starting to apply or be in force from a certain date

ex.en: The new law becomes effective next month.
ex.ko: 그 새 법은 다음 달부터 시행된다.

efficiency.cue: 적은 노력으로 좋은 결과를 내는 정도
efficiency.en: the quality of doing something well with no wasted time or effort

ex.en: The new system improved office efficiency.

efficient.en: working well without wasting time, money, or effort

ex.en: She is an efficient manager.

elbow.en: the joint where your arm bends

ex.en: He hurt his elbow while playing basketball.

elbow.en: push your way somewhere using your elbows

ex.en: He elbowed his way through the crowd.
ex.ko: 그는 팔꿈치로 밀치며 사람들 사이를 헤치고 나아갔다.

election.en: the process of choosing someone for a position by voting

ex.en: The presidential election will be held in November.
There was an electrical problem with the car.

electricity.en: a form of energy used to power lights, machines, and appliances

ex.en: The storm cut off the electricity for hours.
ex.ko: 그 폭풍으로 몇 시간 동안 전기가 끊겼다.

electron.en: a very small particle with a negative electric charge

ex.en: Electrons orbit the nucleus of an atom.

electronic.en: having or using electronic parts or systems

ex.en: She bought a new electronic device.
Trust is an important element of any relationship.

element.cue: 화학에서 더 이상 나눌 수 없는 물질
element.en: a basic chemical substance, such as oxygen or gold, that cannot be broken into simpler substances

ex.en: Oxygen is a chemical element.

eliminate.en: completely remove or get rid of something

ex.en: The new filter eliminates most bacteria.
ex.ko: 그 새 필터는 대부분의 박테리아를 제거한다.

embarrass.en: make someone feel ashamed or uncomfortable

ex.en: His joke embarrassed her in front of everyone.
ex.ko: 그의 농담은 모든 사람 앞에서 그녀를 당황하게 만들었다.

ex.en: I felt embarrassed after tripping in public.
She blushed with embarrassment.

emerge.en: come out or appear from somewhere hidden

ex.en: The sun emerged from behind the clouds.

emotion.en: a strong feeling such as love, fear, or anger

ex.en: She couldn't hide her emotions.
ex.ko: 그녀는 자신의 감정을 숨길 수 없었다.

emotional.en: relating to emotions or showing strong feelings

ex.en: It was an emotional farewell at the airport.

emotionally.en: in a way that relates to feelings

ex.en: She was emotionally exhausted after the trip.
ex.ko: 그녀는 여행 후 감정적으로 지쳐 있었다.

emperor.en: the male ruler of an empire

ex.en: The emperor ruled over a vast territory.
The school puts great emphasis on discipline.
The doctor emphasized the importance of sleep.

empire.en: a group of countries ruled by a single power

ex.en: The Roman Empire lasted for centuries.
ex.ko: 로마 제국은 수 세기 동안 지속되었다.

employment.en: the state of having a paid job

ex.en: She found employment at a local hospital.

enable.en: make it possible for someone to do something

ex.en: This app enables you to track your spending.
ex.ko: 이 앱은 당신이 지출을 추적할 수 있게 해준다.

enclose.en: put something inside an envelope or package along with the main item

ex.en: I have enclosed a copy of my resume.

enclose.en: surround an area completely with a wall, fence, or something similar

ex.en: The garden is enclosed by a tall fence.
ex.ko: 그 정원은 높은 울타리로 둘러싸여 있다.

ex.en: We encountered a few problems along the way.
ex.ko: 우리는 도중에 몇 가지 문제를 마주쳤다.

encounter.en: an unexpected meeting with someone or something

ex.en: It was a strange encounter with an old friend.

encouragement.en: words or actions that give someone confidence or hope

ex.en: She thanked her coach for his encouragement.
The test results were encouraging.

encyclopedia.cue: 여러 주제를 알파벳 순으로 정리한 책
encyclopedia.en: a book or set of books giving information on many subjects

ex.en: He looked up the word in an encyclopedia.

endanger.en: put someone or something at risk of harm

ex.en: Pollution endangers many species.

endless.en: continuing for a very long time or with no end

ex.en: The meeting felt endless.

endure.en: put up with something difficult or painful

ex.en: She endured months of hard training.
ex.ko: 그녀는 몇 달간의 힘든 훈련을 견뎠다.

endure.en: continue to exist over a long time

ex.en: Their friendship has endured for thirty years.

enemy.en: a person or group that is against you

ex.en: The two nations have been enemies for decades.
ex.ko: 그 두 나라는 수십 년 동안 적이었다.

ex.en: The children engaged in a lively discussion.

engage.en: hire someone to do a job

ex.en: They engaged a lawyer to handle the case.
ex.ko: 그들은 그 사건을 맡기려고 변호사를 고용했다.

engaged.en: having formally agreed to marry someone

ex.en: They got engaged last summer.

engaged.en: busy doing something, or (of a phone line) already in use

ex.en: The line is engaged; please try again later.
ex.ko: 통화 중입니다. 나중에 다시 시도해 주세요.

engine.en: a machine that changes energy into movement

ex.en: The car engine wouldn't start this morning.
ex.ko: 오늘 아침 자동차 엔진이 시동이 걸리지 않았다.

engineering.en: the science of designing and building machines, structures, or systems

ex.en: She studied engineering at university.
He is a typical Englishman who loves tea.
ex.ko: 그는 차를 좋아하는 전형적인 영국 남자다.

ex.en: We had an enjoyable evening together.

enjoyment.en: the feeling of pleasure from doing something

ex.en: Reading gives her great enjoyment.
Traveling enriches your understanding of other cultures.
ex.ko: 여행은 다른 문화에 대한 이해를 풍요롭게 한다.

ensure.en: make certain that something will happen

ex.en: Please ensure the door is locked before you leave.
ex.ko: 나가기 전에 문이 잠겼는지 확실히 해 주세요.

entertain.en: provide interesting or fun activities for people

ex.en: The clown entertained the children at the party.
ex.ko: 그 광대는 파티에서 아이들을 즐겁게 해주었다.

entertain.en: consider an idea or possibility seriously

ex.en: He wouldn't even entertain the idea of moving abroad.
ex.ko: 그는 해외로 이주한다는 생각조차 고려하려 하지 않았다.

entertainer.en: a person whose job is to entertain people

ex.en: She started her career as a children's entertainer.

enthusiasm.en: a strong feeling of excitement and interest in something

ex.en: He talked about his new job with great enthusiasm.
ex.ko: 그는 새 직장에 대해 큰 열의를 갖고 이야기했다.

enthusiast.en: a person who is very interested in and enjoys a particular activity

ex.en: He is a keen sports car enthusiast.

enthusiastic.en: showing great excitement and interest in something

ex.en: She is enthusiastic about learning new languages.
ex.ko: 그녀는 새로운 언어를 배우는 것에 열정적이다.

ex.en: He spent the entire day cleaning the house.
I entirely agree with your opinion.

entry.en: the act of going into a place, or the right to go in

ex.en: Entry to the museum is free on Sundays.

entry.en: a single item written in a list, dictionary, or diary

ex.en: She wrote a new entry in her diary.

environmental.en: relating to the natural world and the effect of human activity on it

ex.en: The factory caused serious environmental damage.
ex.ko: 그 공장은 심각한 환경 피해를 일으켰다.

environmentalist.en: a person who works to protect the environment

ex.en: Environmentalists are protesting against the new pipeline.
ex.ko: 환경운동가들이 새 송유관 건설에 반대하고 있다.

equal.en: the same in amount, size, or value

ex.en: All employees should receive equal pay for equal work.
ex.ko: 모든 직원은 동일한 일에 동등한 임금을 받아야 한다.

equal.en: be the same in number or amount as

ex.en: Two plus two equals four.

equality.en: the state of being equal in rights, status, or opportunity

ex.en: The movement fights for gender equality.
ex.ko: 그 운동은 성 평등을 위해 싸운다.

ex.en: The cake was divided equally among the children.

equipment.en: the tools or machines needed for a particular activity

ex.en: The hikers checked their equipment before leaving.

era.en: a period of time marked by particular events or characteristics

ex.en: The internet began a new era of communication.

essence.en: the most basic and important quality of something

ex.en: Honesty is the essence of a good relationship.
Sleep is essential for good health.

establishment.en: the act of starting or creating an organization

ex.en: The establishment of the new hospital took three years.

establishment.en: a business, institution, or building set up for a particular purpose

ex.en: The restaurant is a well-known establishment in town.
ex.ko: 그 식당은 마을에서 잘 알려진 업체다.

estimate.en: guess or calculate the approximate value of something

ex.en: We estimate the trip will take three hours.
ex.ko: 우리는 그 여행이 세 시간 정도 걸릴 것으로 추정한다.

estimate.en: an approximate calculation of an amount or cost

ex.en: The builder gave us an estimate for the repairs.
ex.ko: 그 건축업자는 수리 견적을 우리에게 주었다.

eternal.en: lasting forever or for a very long time

ex.en: They promised each other eternal love.
It felt like an eternity waiting for the results.

evenly.en: in equal parts or in a smooth, regular way

ex.en: Spread the butter evenly over the bread.

eventually.en: after a long time or delay

ex.en: She eventually found her lost keys.
It was evident that she was upset.

exact.en: demand and obtain something, especially by force or authority

ex.en: The kidnappers exacted a huge ransom.
Can you tell me the exact time?

examination.en: a formal test of knowledge or ability

ex.en: She passed the final examination with high marks.

examination.en: a careful check of someone's body or health by a doctor

ex.en: The doctor gave him a thorough examination.

examine.en: look at or check something carefully

ex.en: The doctor examined the patient's throat.

examiner.en: a person who tests or checks someone's knowledge or work

ex.en: The examiner asked her several questions.
ex.ko: 그 시험관은 그녀에게 몇 가지 질문을 했다.

excellence.en: the quality of being extremely good

ex.en: The restaurant is known for its excellence in service.

excess.en: an amount that is more than what is needed or allowed

ex.en: He was charged extra for excess baggage.
ex.ko: 그는 초과 수하물 때문에 추가 요금을 냈다.

exchange rate.cue: 다른 나라 돈으로 바꿀 때의 비율
exchange rate.en: the value of one currency compared to another

ex.en: The exchange rate changes every day.
The kids talked excitedly about the trip.
ex.ko: 아이들은 그 여행에 대해 신나게 이야기했다.

excitement.en: a feeling of great enthusiasm and eagerness

ex.en: There was a lot of excitement before the concert.

exclusive.en: limited to one person or group only

ex.en: The hotel offers exclusive access to the private beach.
ex.ko: 그 호텔은 전용 해변에 대한 독점적인 이용권을 제공한다.

ex.en: She felt exhausted after the long flight.

existence.en: the state of existing or being real

ex.en: Scientists still debate the existence of life on other planets.
ex.ko: 과학자들은 다른 행성의 생명체 존재 여부를 여전히 논쟁한다.

exit.en: a door or way out of a place

ex.en: Please use the emergency exit in case of fire.
ex.ko: 화재 시에는 비상 출구를 이용해 주세요.

exit.en: leave a place or a situation

ex.en: He quietly exited the meeting.

expand.en: become or make larger in size, number, or amount

ex.en: The company plans to expand into new markets.
ex.ko: 그 회사는 새로운 시장으로 확장할 계획이다.

expense.en: the money that you spend on something

ex.en: Housing is our biggest monthly expense.
ex.ko: 주거비는 우리의 가장 큰 월간 지출이다.

experienced.en: having a lot of knowledge or skill from doing something for a long time

ex.en: They hired an experienced pilot for the flight.
ex.ko: 그들은 그 비행을 위해 경험 많은 조종사를 고용했다.

experiment.en: try something new to test what will happen

ex.en: The students experimented with different chemicals.

experiment.en: a scientific test carried out to discover or prove something

ex.en: The scientist conducted a careful experiment in the lab.
ex.ko: 그 과학자는 실험실에서 신중하게 실험을 진행했다.

exploration.en: the activity of traveling to or studying something in order to learn about it

ex.en: Space exploration has advanced rapidly.

explosion.en: a sudden, violent bursting caused by a release of energy

ex.en: The explosion could be heard from miles away.
ex.ko: 그 폭발은 수 킬로미터 밖에서도 들렸다.

expose.en: leave something without protection or covering, especially to a harmful force

ex.en: Don't expose the film to direct sunlight.

expose.en: reveal a secret or dishonest fact to the public

ex.en: The journalist exposed the company's illegal activities.
ex.ko: 그 기자는 그 회사의 불법 행위를 폭로했다.

extend.en: make something longer or larger, in time or space

ex.en: They decided to extend their vacation by two days.
ex.ko: 그들은 휴가를 이틀 더 연장하기로 했다.

extend.en: stretch out a part of your body

ex.en: He extended his hand to greet me.

extensively.en: over a large area or in great detail

ex.en: The topic has been extensively researched.

extent.en: the size or degree of something

ex.en: We don't yet know the extent of the damage.
ex.ko: 우리는 아직 피해의 정도를 알지 못한다.

extinct.en: no longer existing as a species

ex.en: Dinosaurs have been extinct for millions of years.

extinction.en: the state of a species no longer existing

ex.en: Hunting pushed the species to extinction.
She has an extraordinary talent for music.

extreme.en: very great in degree; far beyond what is normal

ex.en: They faced extreme weather conditions on the trip.
ex.ko: 그들은 그 여행에서 극도의 기상 상황을 겪었다.

extreme sports.en: sports that involve high speed, danger, or physical risk

ex.en: Bungee jumping is a popular extreme sport.
ex.ko: 번지 점프는 인기 있는 익스트림 스포츠다.

ex.en: His eyesight has gotten worse with age.

face to face.en: meeting someone in person, not by phone or online

ex.en: They finally talked face to face after months of texting.
ex.ko: 그들은 몇 달간 문자만 주고받다가 마침내 얼굴을 맞대고 이야기했다.

face-to-face.en: involving direct personal contact rather than phone or online

ex.en: We prefer face-to-face meetings over video calls.
ex.ko: 우리는 화상 통화보다 대면 회의를 선호한다.

facility.en: a building or place designed for a particular purpose

ex.en: The gym has excellent sports facilities.
ex.ko: 그 체육관은 훌륭한 스포츠 시설을 갖추고 있다.

facility.en: a natural ability to do something easily

ex.en: She has a facility for learning languages quickly.
ex.ko: 그녀는 언어를 빠르게 배우는 재능이 있다.

fade.en: gradually become less bright, clear, or strong

ex.en: The curtains had faded in the sunlight.

failure.cue: 하려던 일이 잘 안 된 것
failure.en: the fact of not succeeding at something

ex.en: The project ended in complete failure.

failure.en: a person who has not succeeded, especially one who feels ashamed about it

ex.en: He felt like a failure after losing his job.
ex.ko: 그는 직장을 잃고 나서 자신이 실패자처럼 느껴졌다.

faint.en: suddenly lose consciousness for a short time

ex.en: She fainted from the heat.

faint.cue: 잘 보이지도 들리지도 않을 만큼 희미한
faint.en: not strong or clear; barely noticeable

ex.en: I heard a faint sound coming from the basement.

faithful.en: loyal and never letting someone down

ex.en: He remained faithful to his wife.

fake.en: not genuine; made to look like the real thing

ex.en: She was carrying a fake designer bag.
ex.ko: 그녀는 가짜 명품 가방을 들고 있었다.

fake.en: pretend to have a feeling, illness, or quality that you do not really have

ex.en: He faked an injury to avoid playing.
ex.ko: 그는 경기에 나가지 않으려고 부상을 꾸며냈다.

ex.en: The road was blocked by a fallen tree.
ex.ko: 그 길은 쓰러진 나무로 막혀 있었다.

fantasy.en: an imaginary situation or a story with magical or unreal elements

ex.en: The children acted out their own fantasy world.
She dreamed of visiting faraway countries.
ex.ko: 그녀는 멀리 있는 나라들을 방문하는 꿈을 꾸었다.

ex.en: They said their farewells at the station.

farming.en: the activity of growing crops or raising animals

ex.en: His family has been farming this land for generations.
ex.ko: 그의 가족은 대대로 이 땅에서 농사를 지어왔다.

farmland.en: land used for growing crops or raising animals

ex.en: Much of the valley is covered with farmland.
ex.ko: 그 계곡의 대부분은 농지로 덮여 있다.

farther.en: at or to a greater distance (comparative of far)

ex.en: We need to walk a bit farther to reach the lake.
ex.ko: 호수에 도착하려면 조금 더 멀리 걸어야 한다.

farthest.en: at or to the greatest distance (superlative of far)

ex.en: Of all the runners, she went the farthest.
ex.ko: 모든 주자들 중에서 그녀가 가장 멀리 갔다.

fascinate.en: attract and hold someone's attention completely

ex.en: The documentary about space fascinated him.
ex.ko: 그 우주에 관한 다큐멘터리는 그를 매혹시켰다.

fashionable.en: popular or stylish at a particular time

ex.en: Short haircuts are fashionable this year.
Please fasten your seatbelt before takeoff.

favorable.en: showing approval, or likely to bring a good result

ex.en: The reviews of the new restaurant were favorable.
ex.ko: 그 새 식당에 대한 평가는 호의적이었다.

fax.en: a copy of a document sent electronically over a phone line

ex.en: Please send me the contract by fax.

fax.en: send a document using a fax machine

ex.en: Can you fax the form to our office?
ex.ko: 그 서류를 저희 사무실로 팩스 보내주실 수 있나요?

feast.cue: 많은 사람이 모여 먹는 큰 식사
feast.en: a large meal, usually to celebrate something special

ex.en: The wedding feast lasted late into the night.

fellow.en: belonging to the same group, situation, or class as you

ex.en: She shared her lunch with a fellow student.
ex.ko: 그녀는 동료 학생과 점심을 나눠 먹었다.

fellow.en: an informal word for a man

ex.en: Who's that fellow standing by the door?
ex.ko: 저 문 옆에 서 있는 사람은 누구야?

ferry.en: a boat that carries people or vehicles across water on a regular route

ex.en: We took a ferry across the river.

ferry.en: transport people or goods, especially by boat

ex.en: The boat ferries tourists between the islands.
ex.ko: 그 배는 섬들 사이로 관광객들을 실어 나른다.

ex.en: The whole town had a festive mood during the holidays.
ex.ko: 휴일 동안 마을 전체가 축제 분위기였다.

fetch.en: go and bring something or someone back

ex.en: Could you fetch some water from the kitchen?

feverishly.en: in a very energetic and urgent way

ex.en: She worked feverishly to finish the report before the deadline.
ex.ko: 그녀는 마감 전에 보고서를 끝내려고 필사적으로 일했다.

fifth.en: coming after the fourth in a sequence

ex.en: This is the fifth time I've called you today.
ex.ko: 이건 오늘 내가 너에게 전화한 다섯 번째다.

fifth.cue: 전체를 다섯으로 나눈 것 중 하나
fifth.en: one of five equal parts of something

ex.en: Only a fifth of the students passed the exam.

filling.en: the food mixture inside a sandwich, pie, or pastry

ex.en: The pie has a sweet apple filling.
ex.ko: 그 파이에는 달콤한 사과 속이 들어 있다.

filling.en: a substance a dentist uses to fill a hole in a tooth

ex.en: I need to get a filling for my cavity.
ex.ko: 나는 충치 때문에 충전을 받아야 한다.

film-maker.en: a person who makes films, especially as a director or producer

ex.en: She became a successful independent film-maker.
ex.ko: 그녀는 성공한 독립 영화 제작자가 되었다.

filter.en: a device that removes unwanted substances from a liquid, gas, or light

ex.en: You should change the water filter every few months.
ex.ko: 몇 달마다 정수 필터를 교체해야 한다.

filter.en: remove unwanted material by passing something through a filter

ex.en: The machine filters the dust from the air.

finance.en: the management of large amounts of money by governments or companies

ex.en: She works in the finance department of a bank.

finance.en: provide the money needed for something

ex.en: The government agreed to finance the new highway.
ex.ko: 정부는 새 고속도로에 자금을 대기로 합의했다.
